steps.title: Identify the clause structure
steps.content: Left side of the blank: noun phrase acting as subject (Her innovative poems). Right side: noun phrase acting as direct object (the use of Jamaican Creole in literature). Together, they form a single independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the verb-object rule
steps.content:
No punctuation should separate a transitive verb from its direct object.

Choice C follows this rule by placing no punctuation between popularized and the use of Jamaican Creole, keeping the verb-object pair intact.

Choices A, B, and D insert punctuation (colon, comma, semicolon) between the verb and its object, breaking this connection.

This matches choice C — C is correct.
